title: Translational Bioinformatics: From Bed to Bedside
keywords: biomedical informatics, data science, and digital health

summary.hook: This poster is an overview of translational bioinformatics work led by Valentin Dinu (ASU CHS), highlighting data science methods for genomic and clinical data management/analysis and several application areas (biomarkers, pharmacogenomics/substance abuse, and diseases including cancer and Alzheimer’s).
summary.summary_30s: This poster is an overview of translational bioinformatics work led by Valentin Dinu (ASU CHS), highlighting data science methods for genomic and clinical data management/analysis and several application areas (biomarkers, pharmacogenomics/substance abuse, and diseases including cancer and Alzheimer’s). It also emphasizes health disparities and genomics training, plus cloud-based analysis of genomic/proteomic networks, including an approach framed as “Harnessing the power of the Google Search PageRank Algorithm for genomic data analysis.” A set of collaborator logos is included, suggesting a network of research partners.
talktrack_2m: Focus: Present a translational bioinformatics research portfolio and key themes (“From Bed to Bedside”).
Approach: Population: Not stated on poster Primary outcome: Not stated on poster (portfolio overview)
Finding: The poster outlines a translational bioinformatics focus “From Bed to Bedside.”

Why it matters: Authors’ stated conclusions: Not stated on poster (no explicit conclusion section). Implications (poster-grounded): The poster positions translational bioinformatics as enabling movement “from bed to bedside,” using data science methods applied to genomic, clinical, and proteomic/network contexts. It signals interest in both biomedical applications (multiple disease areas) and population/education-oriented aims (health disparities and genomics training). Mechanism/explanation (ONLY if stated): Not stated on poster Connections to prior work (ONLY if referenced): Not stated on poster (some embedded screenshots/figures appear to reference external work, but text is too small to confirm specifics).
takeaways: The poster outlines a translational bioinformatics focus “From Bed to Bedside.”
It highlights data science methods and applications for genomic and clinical data management and analysis.
Research areas include disease biomarkers with modalities such as genomic sequencing, microarrays, metabolomics; and domains including pharmacogenomics and substance abuse.
limitations: The poster does not list formal study limitations.
The poster leaves open this clarification: Is this poster intended to summarize one specific study, or your broader research program/portfolio?

what_to_ask.question: Which disease area listed (cancer, Alzheimer’s, diabetes, AMD, etc.) has the most mature findings or deployments right now?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This gets the presenter to interpret the main result instead of repeating the title.

what_to_ask.question: What validation strategies do you use (external cohorts, cross-site replication, prospective validation)?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This probes confidence, assumptions, or limitations without sounding hostile.

what_to_ask.question: Which parts of your work are closest to clinical implementation today, and what are the remaining barriers?
what_to_ask.why_this_is_good: This opens a useful conversation about why the work matters or where it goes next.
